summary: Matched HSD_ForeachAnim and the needed foreach-animation helper logic in src/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.c, completing the removal of the remaining aobj.s assembly stub from the build. The PR replaces large MUST_MATCH / MWERKS_GEKKO inline assembly blocks and a NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallback with C implementations that dispatch animation callbacks over JObj, DObj, MObj, PObj, TObj, LObj, CObj, RObj, WObj, and Fog object types.
what_changed: Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.s, which had only data symbols for HSD_ForeachAnim panic strings and jump tables.
Removed $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.s.o from obj_files.mk because aobj.c now supplies the matched implementation.
Implemented HSD_ForeachAnim in C instead of leaving it as NOT_IMPLEMENTED / inline assembly guarded by MWERKS_GEKKO.
Replaced the inline assembly implementation of JObjForeachAnim with a static C implementation.
Converted foreach helper routines such as callbackForeachFunc, TObjForeachAnim, RObjForeachAnim, PObjForeachAnim, MObjForeachAnim, and DObjForeachAnim to static file-local helpers.
Added new static traversal helpers for FogForeachAnim, WObjForeachAnim, CObjForeachAnim, and LObjForeachAnim.
Added includes for baselib/cobj.h, baselib/fog.h, baselib/lobj.h, and baselib/wobj.h so the new C traversal code can refer to those object structs and masks directly.
Updated HSD_AObjAlloc to use a local variable named new and HSD_ASSERT(489, new), replacing explicit __assert string globals.
Removed aobj.h prototypes for internal foreach helpers, leaving HSD_ForeachAnim as the public API.
Removed extern declarations for the old assembly-provided strings and jump tables HSD_AObj_8040601C, HSD_AObj_8040603C, jtbl_80406058, and jtbl_8040608C.
smart_moves: Split HSD_ForeachAnim into small static object-specific traversal helpers, making a complex assembly control-flow graph matchable and maintainable in C.
Kept callback invocation centralized in callbackForeachFunc, preserving the common handling of AObj callback argument formats while simplifying per-object traversal.
Used object type and mask constants such as JOBJ_TYPE, DOBJ_TYPE, TOBJ_TYPE, FOG_TYPE, JOBJ_MASK, POBJ_MASK, WOBJ_MASK, and FOG_MASK instead of relying on raw jump table offsets.
Handled C varargs explicitly with va_start, va_arg, and va_end; notably, float-style callback arguments are read as f64, matching C default argument promotion.
Made helper functions static after removing them from the header, reducing exported surface area once the functions were proven to be implementation details.
Replaced raw PObj flag masking with pobj_type(pobj) == POBJ_SHAPEANIM, improving readability while preserving the shape-animation guard used before callback dispatch.
Removed the separate assembly data object only after migrating the panic strings into HSD_Panic calls inside C, avoiding dangling build dependencies.
decomp_lessons: Large dispatcher functions with jump tables can often be recovered as a top-level switch plus small static recursive/list traversal helpers.
When matching variadic functions, account for default promotions: f32-style varargs may need to be consumed with va_arg(ap, f64).
Once a function is matched in C, remember to remove both the asm file and its obj_files.mk entry; otherwise duplicate or stale objects may remain in the link.
Assembly-only labels used for panic messages and jump tables can disappear if the C source naturally emits equivalent string data and switch dispatch.

Recursive scene-graph traversal is easier to match and audit when each object family owns a narrowly scoped helper: JObj recurses over children, DObj over material/polygon objects, WObj over RObj, CObj/LObj over their WObj endpoints, and so on.
Internal decomp helpers should not stay in public headers unless there is evidence other translation units need them.
Use project macros such as HSD_ASSERT and HSD_Panic where they reproduce the original assert/panic behavior and file/line data.
naming_conventions: Object traversal helpers use the pattern <ObjectType>ForeachAnim, e.g. JObjForeachAnim, DObjForeachAnim, MObjForeachAnim, TObjForeachAnim, WObjForeachAnim, CObjForeachAnim, LObjForeachAnim, FogForeachAnim.
Mask/type constants use uppercase object prefixes, e.g. JOBJ_MASK/JOBJ_TYPE, TOBJ_MASK/TOBJ_TYPE, FOG_MASK/FOG_TYPE.
The public API remains HSD_ForeachAnim, while implementation helpers are lowercase/static or object-prefixed static functions.
The allocation local variable was intentionally named new in HSD_AObjAlloc, matching the assert expression HSD_ASSERT(489, new).
PObj shape animation checks prefer pobj_type(pobj) == POBJ_SHAPEANIM over raw flag constants when the helper is available.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed an inline assembly version of JObjForeachAnim and replaced it with C using HSD_ASSERT(715, obj), a JOBJ_MASK callback check, union_type_dobj(obj), RObj traversal, and child recursion gated by !(obj->flags & JOBJ_INSTANCE).
Replaced an inline assembly HSD_ForeachAnim dispatcher with a variadic C function that first normalizes the optional callback argument into callbackArg and then switches on HSD_Type.
The old assembly file contained two jump tables for HSD_ForeachAnim; the C rewrite expresses those dispatches as switch statements over arg_type and type.
Used for-loop traversal forms such as for (; tobj != NULL; tobj = tobj->next) and for (; dobj != NULL; dobj = dobj->next), which likely helped produce compact list-walk code.
Preserved early return for obj == NULL in HSD_ForeachAnim before vararg parsing.
Used HSD_Panic(__FILE__, 826, "unexpected argument format.\n") and HSD_Panic(__FILE__, 862, "unexpected type of object.\n") to replace assembly-referenced panic strings.
Kept PObj callback dispatch guarded by mask & POBJ_MASK, pobj != NULL, pobj_type(pobj) == POBJ_SHAPEANIM, pobj->u.x14_unk != NULL, and pobj->u.x14_unk->aobj != NULL.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching HSD_ForeachAnim or aobj.c to see whether any traversal helper names or masks were corrected.
Search for remaining public prototypes in baselib headers that only serve file-local foreach helpers.
Compare this variadic HSD_ForeachAnim pattern against other decompiled variadic dispatcher functions that require f64 va_arg for promoted floats.
Look for other asm files that only provide jump tables or panic strings after their code has moved into C.
